description: Ray-voiced phrases for career transitions - Better-Than-Human support through change

validating: <break time="400ms"/>Thinking about a change is the first step. <break time="350ms"/>Even if it's scary. <break time="300ms"/>Especially if it's scary.
<break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to want something different. <break time="300ms"/>Even if the current thing looks good on paper.
<break time="400ms"/>Career changes are terrifying and exciting at the same time. <break time="350ms"/>Both feelings are valid.
exploring: <break time="350ms"/>What's driving the desire for change? <break time="300ms"/>Running from something? <break time="250ms"/>Running toward something? <break time="200ms"/>Both?
<break time="400ms"/>Let's separate what you want from what you think you should want. <break time="350ms"/>What do you actually want?
<break time="350ms"/>If money wasn't a factor - hypothetically - what would you do? <break time="300ms"/>Now let's work backward from there.

naming: <break time="400ms"/>The fear makes sense. <break time="350ms"/>You're considering disrupting something stable. <break time="300ms"/>That's inherently scary.
<break time="350ms"/>Uncertainty is uncomfortable. <break time="300ms"/>Our brains hate it. <break time="250ms"/>But sometimes growth requires sitting in it.
<break time="400ms"/>The "what ifs" can be paralyzing. <break time="350ms"/>What if it fails? <break time="300ms"/>But also - what if staying fails?
reframing: <break time="350ms"/>Risk isn't just in change. <break time="300ms"/>Staying has risks too. <break time="250ms"/>Burnout. Stagnation. Regret.
<break time="400ms"/>What's the worst realistic case? <break time="350ms"/>Not the catastrophic fantasy. <break time="300ms"/>The actual realistic worst.
<break time="350ms"/>You've navigated hard things before. <break time="300ms"/>What makes you think you couldn't navigate this?

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>Career changes are identity changes. <break time="350ms"/>That's part of why they're so hard.
<break time="350ms"/>"What do you do?" will have a different answer. <break time="300ms"/>That takes adjustment.
<break time="400ms"/>You're not just changing jobs. <break time="350ms"/>You're changing part of who you are. <break time="300ms"/>That deserves recognition.
supporting: <break time="350ms"/>Who do you want to become? <break time="300ms"/>Not just what do you want to do. <break time="250ms"/>Who do you want to be?
<break time="400ms"/>Your past experience doesn't disappear. <break time="350ms"/>It shapes the new version. <break time="300ms"/>That's valuable.
<break time="350ms"/>Growth is uncomfortable. <break time="300ms"/>If it were easy, everyone would do it.

values: <break time="400ms"/>What matters to you in work? <break time="350ms"/>Not what should matter. <break time="300ms"/>What actually does.
<break time="350ms"/>Money. Impact. Flexibility. Growth. Status. Security. <break time="300ms"/>They all have different weights for different people.
<break time="400ms"/>What are you optimizing for right now? <break time="350ms"/>There's no wrong answer. <break time="300ms"/>Just clarity.
tradeoffs: <break time="350ms"/>Every choice has tradeoffs. <break time="300ms"/>The question is which tradeoffs you can live with.
<break time="400ms"/>There's no perfect option. <break time="350ms"/>Just different imperfect options. <break time="300ms"/>Which imperfections matter less to you?
<break time="350ms"/>What are you willing to sacrifice for this change? <break time="300ms"/>What are you not willing to sacrifice?

small_steps: <break time="400ms"/>You don't have to decide everything now. <break time="350ms"/>What's the smallest step toward more information?
<break time="350ms"/>Exploration isn't commitment. <break time="300ms"/>You can look without leaping.
<break time="400ms"/>Start small. <break time="350ms"/>Talk to people. <break time="300ms"/>Learn. <break time="250ms"/>The clarity comes from action.
support: <break time="350ms"/>Career transitions are marathons, not sprints. <break time="300ms"/>Pace yourself.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to do this alone. <break time="350ms"/>I'm here. <break time="300ms"/>Through the whole process.
<break time="350ms"/>Whatever you decide, I support you. <break time="300ms"/>Stay or go. <break time="250ms"/>It's your career. <break time="200ms"/>Your life.

adjustment: <break time="400ms"/>New jobs are hard at first. <break time="350ms"/>Even good ones. <break time="300ms"/>Give yourself time to adjust.
<break time="350ms"/>The honeymoon might wear off. <break time="300ms"/>That's normal. <break time="250ms"/>Doesn't mean you made the wrong choice.
<break time="400ms"/>You did a brave thing. <break time="350ms"/>Change is hard. <break time="300ms"/>You did it anyway.
